questions.question: What is Typography, Colors & Units and why is it important?
questions.answer: Typography, Colors & Units is a fundamental concept that Font properties, color systems (hex, RGB, HSL), and CSS units. It's important because it forms the foundation for building robust and efficient applications. Understanding Typography, Colors & Units allows developers to make better architectural decisions and write cleaner code.

questions.question: How do you apply Typography, Colors & Units in a real project?
questions.answer: In practice, Typography, Colors & Units is applied by following established patterns and best practices. Start with the simplest implementation, test thoroughly, and iterate. The key is to understand the underlying principles so you can adapt to different scenarios.

questions.question: What are common mistakes when working with Typography, Colors & Units?
questions.answer: Common mistakes include: not understanding the fundamentals before applying advanced patterns, ignoring edge cases, not testing adequately, and over-engineering solutions. The best approach is to start simple and add complexity only when needed.

questions.question: How would you explain Typography, Colors & Units to a beginner?
questions.answer: Typography, Colors & Units is essentially about font properties, color systems (hex, rgb, hsl), and css units. Think of it as a building block — once you understand this concept, you can combine it with other concepts to build complex applications.
